pr.title: Label ASM with more Synth function names
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Labeled a batch of previously unknown HSD Synth/SFX assembly functions before attempting C matching. The PR renamed func_8038xxxx symbols to descriptive HSD_Synth* names in asm/synth.s, updated call sites in asm/AXDriver.s and asm/text_1.s, and synchronized the corresponding commented declarations in include/functions.h. The stated motivation was that these functions were mostly unknown to the community and should be named before matching work starts.
what_changed: Renamed multiple generic Synth assembly function labels to HSD_SynthSFX* names in asm/synth.s.
Updated all visible branch/call sites to use the new labels, including AXDriver calls to HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff, HSD_SynthSFXSetVolumeFade, HSD_SynthSFXCheck, HSD_SynthSFXPause, and HSD_SynthSFXResume.
Updated text_1.s call sites from func_8038AAB0 to HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume and from func_80389084 to HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync.
Updated include/functions.h commented prototype placeholders from anonymous func_8038xxxx names to the new HSD_SynthSFX names.
Promoted lbl_8038ABD4 to a named global HSD_SynthCallback and updated HSD_SynthInit to reference HSD_SynthCallback instead of the raw label.
Changed lbl_8038AD74 to a global func_8038AD74 and updated address materialization sites to reference func_8038AD74.
smart_moves: Renamed symbols at both their definitions and their call/reference sites, preserving assembly addresses and opcodes while improving searchability.
Kept function names aligned with the existing HSD_Synth and HSD_SynthSFX namespace already present nearby, such as HSD_SynthInit, HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflag, and HSD_SynthSFXStopRange.
Updated include/functions.h at the same time as assembly labels, avoiding a mismatch between the function index/header inventory and actual asm labels.
Did not attempt behavioral rewrites or C matching in the same PR; the change was scoped to labeling known symbols first.
decomp_lessons: For decomp setup PRs, labeling unknown assembly functions is useful groundwork even before matching is attempted, especially when community knowledge is sparse.
When renaming an asm function, update direct bl targets, self-branches, address loads using @ha/@l, and header placeholder declarations together.

Descriptive names such as KeyOff, Pause, Resume, Check, UpdatePitch, UpdateVolume, and SetVolumeFade make later function boundary analysis and C matching easier because call-site intent becomes visible.

Callback setter functions can often be named from their simple store-to-SDA behavior plus the callback they are registered with, but this PR does not include explanatory evidence beyond the labels and call sites.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_SynthSFX* for SFX-oriented Synth functions.
Use HSD_Synth* for broader Synth-level callbacks, e.g. HSD_SynthCallback.
Use verb phrases after the subsystem prefix: HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff, HSD_SynthSFXPause, HSD_SynthSFXResume, HSD_SynthSFXCheck.
Use Update* for internal recalculation helpers: HSD_SynthSFXUpdatePitch, HSD_SynthSFXUpdateVolume, HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume.
Use SetDriver*Callback for simple callback registration functions: HSD_SynthSFXSetDriverInactivatedCallback, HSD_SynthSFXSetDriverMasterClockCallback, HSD_SynthSFXSetDriverPauseCallback.
Keep unresolved functions as func_8038xxxx when no stronger name is established, as shown by nearby unchanged placeholders in include/functions.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly-only rename tactic: replace .global func_8038xxxx and the following label with the new symbol while leaving instruction bytes unchanged.
Update recursive/self-branch labels after renaming; func_80389084's busy-wait branch became bne HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync.
Update address materialization pairs such as lis/addi @ha/@l when a raw label becomes a named callback symbol.
Use header placeholder comments as an index of known-but-unmatched functions; the PR changed comments such as // ? func_8038987C(?) to // ? HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff(?).
This PR improves future matching context but contains no C decompilation or nontrivial code-generation tactic evidence.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
searchable_terms: Label ASM with more Synth function names
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or match HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff, HSD_SynthSFXPause, HSD_SynthSFXResume, or HSD_SynthSFXUpdateVolume.
Search for later header moves that replace include/functions.h commented placeholders with typed prototypes for HSD Synth functions.
Check whether HSD_SynthCallback and func_8038AD74 later receive source-level declarations or more precise names.
